title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Inspector Stanley visit the Belgravia home of Nancy Duane, a fashionable artist, to investigate the murders of three guests at the Bluxton Hotel: Harriet Sipper, Edith Gransbury, and Richard Nigg. Nancy, who claims to avoid newspapers to escape misery, reveals that the three victims were her former neighbors in the village of Gleeholm in Pigeon Valley before she moved to London in 1913.

Nancy expresses intense hatred for the victims, stating she had wished them dead many times, but she denies involvement in the killings. Despite her agitation, she allows Stanley to conduct a search of her residence. Regarding her whereabouts during the time of the murders, Nancy provides an alibi, claiming she was at the home of her friend, Louis Wallace, from approximately 6:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. to deliver a portrait and stay for dinner. She notes that Louis's husband, Saint John, is a snobbish artist specializing in plants and fish.

Poirot challenges this alibi by informing Nancy that an eyewitness reported seeing her running out of the Bluxton Hotel at 8:00 p.m. on Thursday in an agitated state, during which she dropped two keys. Nancy dismisses the report as a mistake. The investigation takes a significant turn when Poirot asks if she recognizes the initials PIJ or PJI from Gleeholm; Nancy reacts with visible shock and identifies the initials as belonging to Patrick James Ives, a former clergyman.
